slides.caption: A woman with a Mariposa cigar at a palm-fringed poolside.
A woman with a Mariposa cigar at an open jungle lodge.
A woman with a Mariposa cigar in a Bali pavilion.

position.subline: For those who don't wait for special occasions, but create them.

paragraphs: The story goes that during Cuba's wars of independence, women slipped secret messages to the guerrilleros into the petals of the Mariposa flower, the white butterfly ginger that became the nation's flower in 1936.

Every Mariposa carries a secret of its own. In place of a printed band, a 24-carat gold leaf is laid onto the wrapper by hand. A quiet reminder that the finer things are not for saving. They are meant to be enjoyed today.
lifestyle.closingLine: Don't wait for special occasions. Create them.
